message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_11-41-24_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should apply block-of-3 special premium (60 G) for three alike runes instead of 75 G"
Expected: 71 G (3 runes form a block: 60 G base + 10% + 5 fee = 71)
Current: 88 G (3*25 = 75 base + 10% = 82.5 + 5 = 87.5 ceil = 88)
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_11-41-24_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts

Per spec: "A building block of 3 alike components is offered at a special base premium of 60 G". A "component" type is Rune or Moonstone (insurance value 250 G, premium 25 G each). For exactly 3 alike components, replace 75 G (3*25) with 60 G base premium.

Implement minimally: When grouping items by type, if a type is a component (Rune or Moonstone) and count is exactly 3, base premium is 60 instead of 3*25=75. Note that 4 runes = 100 G (no block), 7 runes = 175 G (no block - block requires exactly 3 per spec).

For now to keep minimal, identify components Rune/Moonstone, group items by type, and for exactly 3 use 60 G.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement minimal code to make it pass.

**Approach**: Minimal grouping logic with a Set of component types and a single conditional for "exactly 3 alike components". 4+ runes still sum at 25 G each (no block applied), per the spec note that the block requires exactly 3.
